question.plain: Integrate x·eˣ using integration by parts

palette.description: Integration by parts: ∫u dv = uv − ∫v du
Result: xe^x − e^x + C = e^x(x−1) + C

steps.description: Choose u = x (differentiates away), dv = eˣdx
Compute du and v from our choices
Apply the integration by parts formula
Verify: product rule on xeˣ gives eˣ + xeˣ; the −eˣ cancels the first eˣ, leaving xeˣ ✓

Power rule on 4x³: multiply by 3, reduce exponent: 4×3=12, 3−1=2 → 12x²

question.plain: Integrate cos(x)sin(x) dx using substitution

Compute: reverse power rule, 1+1=2 for both exponent and denominator
